about.title: Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Trust, Mumbai
about.subtitle: Preserving Heritage, Empowering Future Generations
about.missionText: To serve the Jain community by providing quality education, secure accommodation, and spiritual nourishment to students and travelers, while preserving our rich cultural heritage and fostering values of compassion, non-violence, and selfless service for the betterment of society.
about.empowermentDesc: Providing quality education and accommodation to Jain students, enabling them to pursue academic excellence while staying rooted in their cultural values.
about.spiritualIntegrityDesc: Upholding the principles of Jain philosophy by creating an environment that nurtures spiritual growth, ethical conduct, and disciplined living.
about.serviceDesc: Extending selfless service to the Jain community and society at large through charitable initiatives, community welfare programs, and humanitarian support.
about.aboutPara1: The Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Trust, Mumbai, is a distinguished charitable institution dedicated to the welfare and upliftment of the Jain community. Founded over a century ago by the illustrious Jhaveri family of Bhindar, Rajasthan, the Trust has been a beacon of hope and service for generations.
about.aboutPara2: The Trust manages three key institutions: a Boys Hostel, a Girls Ashram, and a Dharamshala, all located in the heart of Mumbai. These facilities provide affordable and quality accommodation to Jain students pursuing higher education and to pilgrims visiting the city.
about.aboutPara3: With a deep commitment to preserving Jain values of non-violence, truth, and compassion, the Trust has continuously adapted to modern needs while maintaining its cultural roots. The institution operates under the guidance of a dedicated Board of Trustees who ensure transparency, accountability, and adherence to the founding principles.
about.timeline1852Desc: The seeds of the Trust were planted by the visionary Jhaveri family of Bhindar, Rajasthan, who recognized the need for community welfare in Mumbai.
about.timeline1900Desc: The Trust was formally established under the guidance of Seth Hirachand Gumanji, bringing together resources for the betterment of the Jain community.
about.timeline1914Desc: The flagship Boys Hostel was inaugurated on Lamington Road, Mumbai, providing affordable accommodation for Jain students pursuing higher education.
about.timeline1940Desc: Recognizing the importance of women's education, the Girls Ashram was established, offering a safe and nurturing environment for female students.
about.timeline1972Desc: The Dharamshala was opened to serve Jain pilgrims and travelers, providing comfortable lodging for those visiting Mumbai for religious and personal purposes.
about.timelinePresentDesc: Embracing modern technology to streamline hostel management, admissions, and communication while preserving the Trust's core values and heritage.
about.boysHostelDesc: Well-maintained hostel facilities for male students with modern amenities, study rooms, and a supportive community atmosphere.
about.girlsAshramDesc: A secure and nurturing residential facility for female students, equipped with all necessary amenities for comfortable living and focused study.
about.dharamshalaDesc: Comfortable guest accommodations for Jain pilgrims and travelers, located conveniently in Mumbai with easy access to temples and transport.

faqPage.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqPage.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our hostels and services
faqPage.q1: Who can apply for hostel admission?
faqPage.a1: Hostel admission is open to students belonging to the Digambar Jain community who are pursuing their education in Mumbai. Applicants must provide valid community references and proof of enrollment at a recognised educational institution. Both undergraduate and postgraduate students are eligible to apply.
faqPage.a2: The admission process begins with submitting an application through our online portal. After submission, your documents will be verified by the hostel superintendent. Shortlisted applicants are then called for an interview with the trust committee. Upon approval, you will receive an admission offer with instructions for fee payment and room allocation.
faqPage.a3: The trust charges minimal fees to support students from the community. Fee amounts vary depending on the room type (shared or single occupancy) and the specific hostel. Scholarships and fee concessions are available for students demonstrating financial need. Detailed fee structures are shared upon receiving the admission offer.
faqPage.q4: How can I book a room at Dharamshala?
faqPage.a4: Rooms at Hirabaug Dharamshala can be booked through our online portal or by visiting the trust office in person. Advance booking is recommended, especially during festival seasons and peak travel periods. Stays are available for a maximum of 7 days and are open to members of the Jain community with valid identification.
faqPage.q5: Are meals provided in the hostel?
faqPage.a5: Yes, the hostel provides pure Jain vegetarian meals prepared under strict dietary guidelines. The mess operates on a fixed schedule for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Special dietary requirements can be communicated to the warden. No outside non-vegetarian food is permitted on the premises.
faqPage.q6: How can I donate to the trust?
faqPage.a6: Donations to the trust can be made via bank transfer, cheque, or in person at the trust office. All donations are eligible for tax benefits under Section 80G of the Income Tax Act. The trust issues official receipts for all contributions. For large donations or corpus fund contributions, please contact the trust office directly.
faqPage.q7: What facilities are available for girls?
faqPage.a7: The R.R. Shravika Ashram is a dedicated facility for girls with women-only staff and management. The ashram features 24/7 security with CCTV surveillance, a separate study hall, recreation room, and prayer hall. Strict visitor policies ensure a safe and comfortable environment. A female warden resides on the premises at all times.

facilities.title: Facilities & Rules
facilities.subtitle: Guidelines and regulations for our institutions
facilities.boysRule1: All residents must maintain strict discipline and decorum within the hostel premises at all times.
facilities.boysRule2: Entry to the hostel after 9:00 PM is not permitted without prior written permission from the superintendent.
facilities.boysRule3: Consumption of alcohol, tobacco, or any intoxicating substances is strictly prohibited and will result in immediate expulsion.
facilities.boysRule4: Visitors are allowed only in the designated visitor area during visiting hours (4:00 PM to 7:00 PM on weekends).
facilities.boysRule5: Residents are responsible for maintaining cleanliness of their rooms and common areas as per the assigned duty roster.
facilities.boysRule6: Use of personal electrical appliances such as heaters, cookers, or irons is not permitted in the rooms without approval.
facilities.boysRule7: All residents are expected to attend the morning prayer assembly held daily at 6:30 AM in the prayer hall.
facilities.boysRule8: Any damage to hostel property will be charged to the resident responsible, and repeated offences may lead to disciplinary action.
facilities.girlsRule1: Entry to the ashram after 8:00 PM is not permitted. Late entry requires prior written consent from the warden.
facilities.girlsRule2: Male visitors are not allowed beyond the reception area. All meetings must take place in the designated visitor room.
facilities.girlsRule3: Overnight stays outside the ashram require written consent from a parent or guardian, submitted to the warden at least 24 hours in advance.
facilities.girlsRule4: Residents must inform the warden before leaving the ashram premises, stating the destination and expected time of return.
facilities.girlsRule5: A modest dress code must be followed within the ashram premises as per the guidelines issued by the management.
facilities.girlsRule6: Mobile phone usage is restricted during study hours (7:00 PM to 9:30 PM) and during prayer sessions.
facilities.girlsRule7: All residents are expected to participate in cultural and religious events organised by the ashram and the trust.
facilities.dharamshalaRule1: Check-in time is 12:00 PM and check-out time is 11:00 AM. Early check-in or late check-out is subject to availability.
facilities.dharamshalaRule2: Valid government-issued photo ID proof is mandatory for all guests at the time of check-in.
facilities.dharamshalaRule3: Maximum duration of stay is 7 days. Extensions may be granted in exceptional circumstances with approval from the trust office.
facilities.dharamshalaRule4: Non-vegetarian food, alcohol, and tobacco products are strictly prohibited within the dharamshala premises.
facilities.dharamshalaRule5: Silence must be maintained during prayer hours (6:00 AM to 7:00 AM and 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM).
facilities.dharamshalaRule6: Cancellations must be made at least 24 hours before the check-in date to receive a full refund of the advance payment.
facilities.dharamshalaRule7: Vehicle parking is available on a first-come, first-served basis. The management is not responsible for any loss or damage to vehicles.

news.title: News & Announcements
news.subtitle: Stay updated with the latest happenings at our institutions
news.item1Desc: Applications are now being accepted for the upcoming academic year. Apply online through our streamlined admission process for Boys Hostel, Girls Ashram, and Dharamshala.
news.item2Desc: The hostel community came together to celebrate Paryushan Mahaparva with devotion and enthusiasm. Events included special prayers, lectures, and community gatherings.
news.item3Desc: Over 200 alumni gathered for the annual reunion, sharing memories and experiences. The event featured cultural programs, felicitations, and mentorship sessions for current residents.
news.item4Desc: A state-of-the-art library wing has been inaugurated with over 5,000 new books, digital resources, and dedicated study spaces for residents.
news.item5Desc: The trust has expanded its scholarship program to support more deserving students. New categories include merit-based, need-based, and sports scholarships.

trustees.title: Board of Trustees
trustees.heritageSubtitle: A legacy of service, faith, and dedication spanning over a century and a half.
trustees.keyManagementSubtitle: The dedicated leaders who guide the Trust with wisdom and commitment.
trustees.livingLegacyText: The Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Trust stands as a testament to the enduring power of faith, service, and community. From its origins in the small town of Bhindar to its present-day impact in Mumbai, the Trust continues to honor its founders' vision by empowering students, supporting pilgrims, and preserving the rich cultural heritage of the Jain community. As it embraces digital transformation, the Trust remains steadfast in its commitment to the values that have guided it for over 170 years.
trustees.heritage1Desc: The Jhaveri family traces its roots to the historic town of Bhindar in Rajasthan, a region renowned for its rich Jain heritage and tradition of philanthropy. From these humble beginnings, the family established itself as a pillar of the community.
trustees.heritage2Desc: The early patriarchs of the Jhaveri family laid the groundwork for what would become one of Mumbai's most respected charitable trusts. Their vision of community service and educational empowerment continues to guide the Trust's mission.
trustees.heritage3Desc: Seth Hirachand Gumanji, whose name the Trust proudly bears, was a visionary leader who dedicated his life and resources to the welfare of the Jain community. His legacy of generosity and compassion remains the cornerstone of the institution.
trustees.heritage4Desc: Across multiple generations, the Jhaveri family has maintained an unwavering commitment to the Trust's founding principles. Each generation has built upon the work of its predecessors, expanding the reach and impact of the Trust's charitable activities.
trustees.member1Desc: Seema Javeri has been leading the Trust's operations with dedication and vision for over a decade. Under her stewardship, the Trust has modernized its facilities, streamlined admissions processes, and strengthened its commitment to student welfare while preserving the institution's core values.
trustees.member2Desc: Asit Jhaveri brings a wealth of experience in governance and community development to the Board of Trustees. His strategic guidance has been instrumental in the Trust's growth, particularly in expanding educational programs and ensuring financial sustainability for future generations.

dpdpPolicy.title: Data Protection & Privacy Policy
dpdpPolicy.subtitle: Data Protection and Privacy Principles (DPDP) Act Compliance
dpdpPolicy.privacyBanner: Your privacy rights are protected under Indian law. This policy outlines how we collect, use, and protect your data.
dpdpPolicy.weCollectDesc: Personal, academic, and financial data for hostel management
dpdpPolicy.weUseDesc: Only for admission, stay management, safety, and compliance
dpdpPolicy.weKeepDesc: During stay + 1-7 years based on data type
dpdpPolicy.policyUpdatesText: This policy was last updated on January 1, 2025. We may update this policy periodically to reflect changes in practices or regulations.
dpdpPolicy.consentRenewal: For students, consent renewal is required every 6 months as part of the renewal process. You will be notified when updates require new consent.
dpdpPolicy.policyQuestions: For questions about this policy, contact the Data Protection Officer
dpdpPolicy.dataCollectionContent:
We collect the following personal information for hostel management purposes:

• Personal Identification: Full name, date of birth, gender, photograph
• Contact Information: Residential address, phone number, email address
• Academic Records: Institution name, course/year, enrollment number
• Financial Information: Bank account details, payment history, fee receipts
• Medical Information: Health conditions, allergies, emergency contacts (voluntary)
• Biometric Data: Fingerprint/face recognition for attendance (optional, with explicit consent)
dpdpPolicy.dataUsageContent:
Your data is used exclusively for the following purposes:

• Hostel Admission and Management: Processing applications, room allocation, and stay management
• Academic Coordination: Communication with educational institutions
• Fee Processing: Payment collection, receipt generation, and financial records
• Safety and Security: Emergency contacts, visitor management, and attendance tracking
• Compliance: Meeting legal and regulatory requirements
• Communication: Sending important notices about hostel matters

We do not use your data for any purpose beyond these stated objectives without your explicit consent.
dpdpPolicy.dataSharingContent:
Your personal data may be shared with the following entities under strict conditions:

• Institutional Authorities: Hostel administration and management committees
• Parents/Guardians: For communication and fee matters (with student consent)
• Government Agencies: As required by law (e.g., police, local administration)
• Service Providers: Mess contractors, security services, maintenance providers
• Educational Institutions: For academic coordination and verification

We ensure all third parties adhere to data protection standards through contractual agreements.
dpdpPolicy.dataRetentionContent:
We retain your personal data for the following periods:

• Active Stay: Throughout your period of stay at the hostel
• Alumni Records: 1 year after exit for institutional records
• Financial Records: 7 years as per legal and tax requirements
• Application Records: 1 year after rejection or withdrawal
• Consent Records: Permanent (withdrawals noted) for audit purposes

After retention periods, your data is securely deleted or anonymized.
dpdpPolicy.yourRightsContent:
Under the Data Protection and Privacy Principles (DPDP) Act, you have the following rights:

• Right to Access: Request a copy of all personal data we hold about you
• Right to Rectification: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data
• Right to Erasure: Request deletion of your personal data (subject to legal requirements)
• Right to Data Portability: Request your data in a structured, commonly used format
• Right to Withdraw Consent: Withdraw any consent given, with notice that it may affect services
• Right to Lodge Complaints: File complaints with the Data Protection Board

To exercise these rights, contact the Data Protection Officer.
dpdpPolicy.contactComplaintsContent:
Data Protection Officer Contact:

Email: dpo@hostelmanagement.in
Phone: +91-XXX-XXXX-XXXX
Address: [Institution Address]

For complaints, you may also contact:
Data Protection Board of India
Website: https://dpbi.gov.in

boysHostel.title: Boys' Hostel
boysHostel.subtitle: Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Hostel
boysHostel.overview: The Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Hostel has been a home away from home for Digambar Jain students pursuing higher education in Mumbai for decades. Located in the heritage precinct of Hirabaug, the hostel provides a disciplined, nurturing, and community-oriented environment that supports academic excellence and personal growth. With well-maintained facilities and a strong emphasis on Jain values, the hostel ensures residents thrive in a safe and structured setting.
boysHostel.facility1Desc: Well-furnished single and shared rooms with beds, study desks, and storage
boysHostel.facility2Desc: High-speed internet access available throughout the hostel premises
boysHostel.facility3Desc: Dedicated quiet study hall open 24 hours for focused academic work
boysHostel.facility4Desc: Pure Jain vegetarian meals served three times daily in a spacious dining area
boysHostel.facility5Desc: 24/7 hot water availability in all bathrooms and washing areas
boysHostel.facility6Desc: Round-the-clock security with CCTV surveillance and controlled entry
boysHostel.eligibility1: Must belong to the Digambar Jain community
boysHostel.eligibility2: Must be enrolled at a recognised educational institution in Mumbai
boysHostel.eligibility3: Age between 17 and 25 years at the time of admission
boysHostel.eligibility4: Must provide two community references from known Jain families
boysHostel.eligibility5: Must agree to abide by the hostel rules and code of conduct

girlsHostel.title: Girls' Hostel
girlsHostel.subtitle: R. R. Shravika Ashram
girlsHostel.overview: The R. R. Shravika Ashram is a dedicated residential facility for young women of the Digambar Jain community pursuing education in Mumbai. The ashram is managed entirely by women staff and provides a secure, empowering, and culturally rich environment. With 24/7 security, women-only management, and a focus on holistic development, the ashram ensures that every resident can focus on her education while staying connected to her roots and values.
girlsHostel.facility1Desc: Secure, well-furnished rooms with individual lockers and comfortable bedding
girlsHostel.facility2Desc: Women security guards, CCTV surveillance, and biometric access control
girlsHostel.facility3Desc: Well-stocked library and dedicated study spaces for academic pursuits
girlsHostel.facility4Desc: Nutritious Jain vegetarian meals prepared by trained kitchen staff
girlsHostel.facility5Desc: First aid facility and tie-up with nearby hospitals for medical emergencies
girlsHostel.facility6Desc: Common area for cultural activities, yoga sessions, and community gatherings
girlsHostel.eligibility1: Must belong to the Digambar Jain community
girlsHostel.eligibility2: Must be a female student enrolled at a recognised institution in Mumbai
girlsHostel.eligibility3: Age between 17 and 25 years at the time of admission
girlsHostel.eligibility4: Written consent from parent or guardian is mandatory
girlsHostel.eligibility5: Must provide two community references from known Jain families
girlsHostel.eligibility6: Must agree to the ashram code of conduct and rules
girlsHostel.location: R.R. Shravika Ashram, Near Hirabaug, Mumbai - 400014

dharamshala.title: Dharamshala
dharamshala.subtitle: Hirabaug
dharamshala.overview: The Hirabaug Dharamshala offers comfortable and affordable accommodation for Jain community members visiting Mumbai for religious, medical, or personal purposes. Situated in the historic Hirabaug compound, the dharamshala is conveniently located near major temples, hospitals, and transport hubs. Whether you are visiting for a religious pilgrimage, medical treatment, or family events, the dharamshala provides a clean, peaceful, and welcoming stay.
dharamshala.facility1Desc: Clean and comfortable rooms available in single, double, and family configurations
dharamshala.facility2Desc: Spacious parking facility available for guests on a first-come basis
dharamshala.facility3Desc: Pure Jain vegetarian meals available on pre-order basis for all guests
dharamshala.facility4Desc: Complimentary internet access in common areas and select rooms
dharamshala.facility5Desc: 24/7 hot water supply in all rooms and shared bathroom facilities
dharamshala.facility6Desc: Staffed reception desk and security available round the clock
dharamshala.eligibility1: Open to members of the Jain community with valid identification
dharamshala.eligibility2: Government-issued photo ID proof is mandatory at check-in
dharamshala.eligibility3: Maximum stay duration of 7 days per booking
dharamshala.eligibility4: Advance booking recommended during festival and peak seasons
dharamshala.eligibility5: Guests must adhere to the dharamshala code of conduct
dharamshala.location: Hirabaug Dharamshala, Dr. B.A. Road, Mumbai - 400014
dharamshala.hours: Reception: 24 hours | Office: Mon-Sat, 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM

success.dharamshalaStep1: Our team will review your application within 1-2 business days.
success.dharamshalaStep2: You will receive an SMS/email notification about your booking confirmation.
success.dharamshalaStep3: Upon approval, you will receive check-in instructions and payment details.
